methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kvertus is a Growth-stage frontier-tech company in Defense & Security founded by Yaroslav Filimonov, currently raising capital with approximately $130M deployed to date. The sector comprises 21 public and private peers, though only 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers exist within accessible data. Notable private peers include Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Kvertus. The analysis identified zero same-stage peers, one adjacent-stage peer, and zero similar-tier comparables—falling below the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for robust median analysis. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable distortion given the heterogeneity of Defense & Security subsectors and company maturity profiles. Public comparables DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS offer limited directional insight but insufficient precision for triangulation given differences in geography, technology stack, and revenue scale.

Without meeting quantitative thresholds for peer-median or full triangulation, a supported valuation range cannot be derived from the available data. Further analysis would require either (1) access to confidential valuations or recent funding rounds of the top private peer set, or (2) fundamental metrics (revenue, gross margin, growth rate) enabling revenue multiple or DCF approaches. The key uncertainty remains the scarcity of comparable exits and late-stage funding rounds in this narrowly-defined subsector.
